subject: Sports
title: The Physics of a Perfect Jump
description: A quick, practical guide to The Physics of a Perfect Jump.

hook.text: High jumpers arch their backs and clear the bar while their center of mass passes underneath it.

buildup.text: Dick Fosbury revolutionized the high jump in 1968 by going over the bar backward—the 'Fosbury Flop.'

discovery.text: The flop works because arching the back lets the body clear the bar while the center of gravity stays below it.

twist.text: Before Fosbury, jumpers used the straddle technique. Within a decade, everyone copied his flop.

climax.text: This single biomechanical insight raised world records by several inches over the following decades.

punchline.text: Go over the bar while your center of mass goes under. Pure physics.

quiz.question: What makes the Fosbury Flop so effective?
options: The body clears the bar while the center of gravity passes below it
It requires more speed than other techniques
The athlete jumps from both feet
